vendors.name: Obat
vendors.tagline: Devis-facture et suivi de chantier, pensé pour le bâtiment
vendors.market_position: A French-built quote-and-invoice (devis-facture) and worksite-tracking tool made specifically for building trades, with transparent pricing including a dedicated micro-entreprise tier — one of the cleanest BTP fits for a solo artisan or small crew.
pros: French-native, built for the BTP niche
Published pricing incl. a micro-entreprise tier
cons: Smaller than the big ERP publishers
Site/chantier depth lighter than heavy ERPs
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: micro from €32/mo HT; Pro €49, Croissance €79, Booster €129/mo HT.
vendors.fr_specific_note: A strong default for a French artisan who wants devis-facture done properly with the right TVA rates — and the micro-entreprise tier matches how most tradespeople are actually set up.

vendors.name: Tolteck
vendors.tagline: Devis & factures simples, conçus avec des artisans (et hors-ligne)
vendors.market_position: A French devis-and-invoice tool built with artisans, that works offline on site and is backed by Saint-Gobain Distribution Bâtiment France — simple, fast quoting for tradespeople who hate admin.
pros: Made with artisans; very simple to use
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: €25/mo HT (or €19/mo HT billed annually), unlimited users.
vendors.fr_specific_note: The pick for an artisan who wants the fastest possible devis with the least friction — and the offline mode genuinely matters on sites with no signal.

vendors.name: EBP Bâtiment
vendors.tagline: Devis, facture et chantier d’un éditeur français établi
vendors.market_position: The building edition of EBP, a long-established French business-software publisher — devis, invoicing and worksite management with the reassurance of a large local vendor and accountant familiarity.
cons: Routes e-invoicing via Cegid’s platform (not its own PDP)
More setup than a simple quoting app
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: Bâtiment ACTIV from €53/mo HT (1 user).
vendors.fr_specific_note: A safe established-vendor choice, especially if your accountant already works in EBP — just note its e-invoicing runs through Cegid’s platform rather than its own.

vendors.name: Batappli
vendors.tagline: Logiciel BTP tout-en-un avec la bibliothèque Batichiffrage
vendors.market_position: A French all-in-one BTP tool (publisher AGIRIS) with the Batichiffrage price library built in — useful for artisans who want ready-made priced works items rather than building every devis line from scratch.
cons: Interface less modern than newer tools
Library value depends on your trade
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: Devis €39, Artisan €79, Entreprise €119 per user/mo HT.
vendors.fr_specific_note: The Batichiffrage library is the draw — if pricing works items accurately is your pain point, Batappli saves real time on every devis.

vendors.name: Mediabat
vendors.tagline: Devis, facturation et chantier pour le BTP
vendors.market_position: A French quoting/invoicing/worksite tool for building trades with affordable published tiers — a workable mid-market option for small BTP firms.
vendors.fr_specific_note: A budget-friendly French BTP option worth shortlisting against Obat and Mediabat’s peers if price is the deciding factor.

vendors.name: Vertuoza
vendors.tagline: Gestion tout-en-un bâtiment, avec saisie vocale par IA
vendors.market_position: An all-in-one construction-management tool (quotes, invoicing, worksite) with AI voice input, serving the French market from Belgium — modern and feature-rich, though pricing is quote-only and it’s not French-native.
vendors.fr_specific_note: Capable and modern, but it’s a Belgian product serving France — confirm its French e-invoicing (PDP) readiness and get a written quote before committing.

vendors.name: Organilog
vendors.tagline: Gestion d’interventions et de maintenance sur le terrain
vendors.market_position: A French field-intervention and maintenance-management tool — strongest for trades doing recurring service/maintenance rounds (plumbing, HVAC, facilities) rather than one-off construction projects.
pros: Free tier to start; clear per-user pricing
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: free tier; Pro €19, Business €35, Premium €59 per user/mo HT.
vendors.fr_specific_note: The pick for service/maintenance-led trades (chauffagistes, plombiers on contracts) rather than project builders — pair with a devis tool if you also quote big jobs.

vendors.name: Axonaut
vendors.tagline: Gestion d’entreprise tout-en-un (facturation, CRM, trésorerie)
vendors.market_position: A French all-in-one small-business tool (invoicing, CRM, treasury) — not BTP-specific, but a solid general option for an artisan business that wants CRM and cash-flow alongside invoicing.
pros: Good for the business side beyond devis
vendors.fr_specific_note: Better if you want CRM and treasury management than if you need BTP-specific quoting — many artisans pair a devis tool with general business software, and this is the latter.

vendors.name: Habitatpresto
vendors.tagline: Mise en relation particuliers ↔ artisans vérifiés
vendors.market_position: A leading French renovation lead-gen brand connecting homeowners to vetted artisans on a subscription model — no per-devis charge and no commission on the work, which makes cost predictable.
vendors.fr_specific_note: The cleanest French renovation lead channel to try first — the no-commission subscription means a busy month doesn’t cost you more. Ask for cost-per-signed-job data before committing.

vendors.name: Quotatis
vendors.tagline: Mise en relation travaux, adossée au groupe ADEO (Leroy Merlin)
vendors.market_position: A French homeowner-to-artisan matching platform backed by Groupe ADEO (Leroy Merlin), also handling product-installation dispatch — strong consumer reach through its retail parent.
vendors.fr_specific_note: Worth testing alongside Habitatpresto — the Leroy Merlin connection drives real consumer volume, but measure cost-per-won-job since the model isn’t transparent upfront.

vendors.name: Travaux.com
vendors.tagline: Place de marché travaux (propriété US — groupe Angi/Instapro)
vendors.market_position: A large French-origin renovation marketplace now owned by the US-listed Angi (via Instapro), connecting homeowners and artisans on a pay-per-lead model with free registration.
vendors.fr_specific_note: Big reach, but it’s US-owned and pay-per-lead — set a strict budget and track cost-per-job, because shared leads can get expensive fast.

vendors.name: StarOfService
vendors.tagline: Place de marché de services locaux (rénovation incluse)
vendors.market_position: A French (Paris) local-services marketplace where renovation is one vertical among many, on a credit / pay-per-quote model with optional subscription — broad but less BTP-focused than the pure renovation platforms.
vendors.typical_pricing: Credit / pay-per-quote + optional subscription (not clearly public).
vendors.fr_specific_note: Generalist rather than BTP-specialised — fine as a secondary channel, but the renovation-focused platforms usually convert better for building trades.

vendors.name: Allotravaux
vendors.tagline: Mise en relation travaux & rénovation (angle RGE/énergie)
vendors.market_position: A French homeowner-to-artisan renovation platform with an energy-renovation/RGE angle, on a pay-per-lead model — relevant if your work skews toward subsidised energy-renovation jobs.
cons: Brand split (.com vs allo-travaux.fr) is confusing
vendors.fr_specific_note: Most useful if you’re RGE-qualified and chasing energy-renovation work — confirm which domain/brand you’re actually signing with, as the naming is ambiguous.

vendors.name: PayFit
vendors.tagline: Paie + RH en ligne (bulletins, DSN, congés) pour TPE/PME
vendors.market_position: A French cloud payroll and HR platform (payslips, DSN declarations, leave) sold directly to small businesses — the most accessible self-serve option, and the only payroll vendor here with full public pricing.
cons: Less suited to very large headcounts
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: Starter from €26/mo HT per employee + base subscription; 30-day trial.
vendors.fr_specific_note: The easiest payroll to set up yourself. One caveat for builders: confirm it handles BTP-specific items (caisses de congés CIBTP, intempéries) for your situation before switching.

vendors.name: Silae
vendors.tagline: Moteur de paie dominant, via experts-comptables
vendors.market_position: The dominant French payroll engine by volume (millions of payslips monthly), with 900+ collective agreements — but sold through accountants (experts-comptables), not direct, so you access it via your firm.
cons: Less hands-on for the business owner
vendors.fr_specific_note: If you already use an expert-comptable, you may effectively be on Silae — its BTP convention coverage is excellent. It’s the accountant-led route rather than a DIY tool.

vendors.name: EBP Paie
vendors.tagline: Paie et déclarations sociales pour TPE/PME
vendors.market_position: The payroll module from French publisher EBP — payroll and social declarations for small firms, a natural fit if you already run EBP Bâtiment for your devis and invoicing.
pros: French publisher; integrates with EBP suite
cons: Best value inside the EBP ecosystem
vendors.fr_specific_note: Most compelling if you already run EBP Bâtiment — keeping devis, invoicing and payroll in one publisher reduces friction. Otherwise PayFit is the easier standalone.

vendors.name: Sage Paie
vendors.tagline: Paie en ligne pour petites entreprises
vendors.market_position: Sage’s French online payroll for small businesses — the one non-French-founded payroll option here, with the reassurance of a global brand and a large accountant ecosystem.
vendors.typical_pricing: Reportedly from ~€24/mo HT (entry); confirm with Sage.
vendors.fr_specific_note: A credible global-brand alternative to the French-native options — but for a small French artisan, PayFit’s self-serve simplicity usually wins unless your accountant standardises on Sage.

vendors.name: SMABTP
vendors.tagline: L’assureur mutualiste historique du BTP — décennale + RC Pro
vendors.market_position: The historic French BTP mutual insurer (since 1859) and the reference for artisan décennale — bundling mandatory ten-year decennial liability with RC Pro, with deep building-sector specialism.
vendors.fr_specific_note: The incumbent every French builder knows for décennale. Get a SMABTP quote as your benchmark, then compare the insurtechs (Orus, April) on price and speed.

vendors.name: April Construction
vendors.tagline: RC Décennale sur mesure par métier, prix « à partir de » publiés
vendors.market_position: A French insurer/broker offering décennale tailored per trade (up to 10 activities) — and one of the few publishing indicative "à partir de" pricing, which makes it easy to benchmark.
cons: Final price still needs a quote
vendors.typical_pricing: Published "à partir de": électricien from €69 TTC/mo; peintre from €72/mo.
vendors.fr_specific_note: A strong décennale benchmark precisely because it publishes starting prices — useful to sanity-check a SMABTP or AXA quote before you sign.

vendors.name: Orus
vendors.tagline: Décennale 100% en ligne, attestation sous 24h
vendors.market_position: A French insurtech offering fully online décennale across 100+ BTP trades with an attestation issued within 24 hours and published starting prices — the fast, modern route to mandatory cover.
cons: Cover depth vs specialists to compare
vendors.fr_specific_note: The quickest way to get compliant décennale cover if you need an attestation fast — just compare its cover scope against SMABTP for bigger or higher-risk work.

vendors.name: AXA (BATISSUR)
vendors.tagline: Décennale et multirisque BTP (offre BATISSUR)
vendors.market_position: AXA’s building offer (BATISSUR) provides décennale and multirisk BTP cover with the backing of a major global insurer — a mainstream alternative to the BTP mutuals.
vendors.fr_specific_note: A solid mainstream option, especially if you already bank/insure with AXA — but benchmark it against the BTP specialists (SMABTP) and the insurtechs (Orus/April) on both price and cover.

vendors.name: MAAF Pro
vendors.tagline: Mutuelle généraliste avec solutions décennale artisan
vendors.market_position: A French generalist mutual (Covéa group) offering artisan décennale solutions — a familiar mainstream name for tradespeople who want one insurer across pro and personal lines.
vendors.fr_specific_note: Convenient if you want pro and personal cover under one familiar mutual — but for décennale specifically, benchmark against the BTP specialists before deciding.

vendors.name: Facturation électronique (e-invoicing)
vendors.tagline: Facturation électronique obligatoire via une plateforme agréée (PDP)
vendors.market_position: France is moving all B2B invoicing onto government-regulated structured e-invoicing through approved platforms (PDP / "plateformes agréées"): reception becomes mandatory for ALL businesses on 1 September 2026, and issuance reaches SMEs and micro-entreprises (most artisans) on 1 September 2027.
cons: Reception mandatory for ALL by 1 Sept 2026
Issuance mandatory for artisans by 1 Sept 2027
You must use an approved platform (PDP)
The free public portal (PPF) was scrapped — no free issuance route
vendors.fr_specific_note: The defining French software change: from Sept 2026 you must be able to RECEIVE e-invoices, and from Sept 2027 you must ISSUE them via a PDP. See our facturation-électronique guide for the registered platforms. The free public portal was abandoned — every business needs a PDP.

vendors.name: Assurance décennale (Loi Spinetta)
vendors.tagline: Assurance responsabilité décennale — obligatoire avant tout chantier
vendors.market_position: Mandatory ten-year decennial-liability insurance under the Loi Spinetta (Code des assurances L241-1), taken out BEFORE a worksite opens. It covers, for 10 years, damage compromising the structure’s solidity or making it unfit for purpose — and working without it is a criminal offence.
pros: Legally mandatory — a market entry requirement
cons: Must be in place before the worksite opens
Per-activity cover scope to get right
vendors.fr_specific_note: Non-negotiable for French building work — and uniquely central: French clients expect your décennale insurer and policy number on the quote. Get cover before you start, not after.

vendors.name: RGE (Reconnu Garant de l’Environnement)
vendors.tagline: Label qualité — indispensable pour les aides à la rénovation énergétique
vendors.market_position: A quality label (awarded by Qualibat, Qualit’EnR, Qualifelec and others) that isn’t legally required to operate — but is commercially essential, because the homeowner can only claim MaPrimeRénov’, CEE or éco-PTZ subsidies if the installer holds the relevant RGE qualification.
cons: Not mandatory to operate (but you lose subsidised work)
vendors.fr_specific_note: In the subsidy-driven renovation market keeping the sector afloat, RGE is effectively the price of entry: no RGE means your clients can’t get the aid, so they’ll pick an installer who has it.

vendors.name: TVA bâtiment (20 / 10 / 5,5 %)
vendors.tagline: TVA à taux multiples — 20% standard, 10% rénovation, 5,5% énergie
vendors.market_position: French building work uses multiple VAT rates: 20% standard, a reduced 10% for renovation/improvement, and 5.5% for energy-renovation work — applicable to dwellings over two years old, with conditions, and (since March 2025) excluding fossil-fuel boilers.
pros: Reduced rates lower client cost (sales advantage)
Built into good French BTP software
vendors.fr_specific_note: Your software must apply 20/10/5.5 correctly per line and carry the rate-justification mention on the devis — getting the reduced rate right is both a compliance duty and a competitive price advantage.

vendors.name: URSSAF & régime micro-entrepreneur
vendors.tagline: Cotisations sociales et régime auto-entrepreneur
vendors.market_position: URSSAF collects social contributions (cotisations sociales). A large share of French tradespeople operate as micro-entrepreneurs — turnover-based contributions, simplified accounting and (below the threshold) a VAT franchise — declared via the URSSAF auto-entrepreneur portal.
cons: Franchise invoices need the art. 293 B mention
vendors.fr_specific_note: Most solo French artisans are micro-entrepreneurs, so your software must support both the micro regime (incl. the "TVA non applicable, art. 293 B" mention) and full-VAT sociétés as you grow.

vendors.name: Immatriculation CMA (guichet unique / RNE)
vendors.tagline: Immatriculation de l’entreprise artisanale (guichet unique → RNE → SIRET)
vendors.market_position: Registering an artisan business goes through the INPI guichet unique into the Registre National des Entreprises (RNE), producing a SIRET; the Chambre de Métiers et de l’Artisanat (CMA) validates artisanal qualification. Registration is mandatory.
vendors.fr_specific_note: Your SIRET from this registration is what lets you legally invoice — and for regulated trades the CMA will check your qualification (diploma or 3 years’ experience).

vendors.name: Qualification professionnelle (Code de l’artisanat)
vendors.tagline: Diplôme ou 3 ans d’expérience pour les métiers réglementés
vendors.market_position: Several building trades are regulated: you must hold a relevant qualification (a CAP/BP diploma) OR prove three years’ professional experience to run the business, under the Code de l’artisanat (L121-1 to L121-3).
vendors.fr_specific_note: For regulated trades (e.g. électricien, plombier doing building work), you must show the qualification at registration — the experience route (3 years) exists if you don’t hold the diploma.

vendors.name: Qualibat
vendors.tagline: Qualification/certification volontaire — et organisme RGE de référence
vendors.market_position: A voluntary French qualification and certification body for building firms — not legally required, but a recognised quality mark and one of the leading bodies that awards RGE, so often de-facto needed for larger or subsidised work.
cons: Value depends on your work type
vendors.fr_specific_note: Voluntary, but valuable: a Qualibat qualification is a trust signal, and Qualibat is a main route to RGE — so it often pays for itself on subsidised renovation work.
